svg.text: Experiment Pipeline
From source documents to quantitative analysis, the workflow separates expensive indexing from cheap repeatable evaluation.
1. Data acquisition
Download Treasury Bulletin PDFs referenced by the OfficeQA CSV, verify source files, and build a shared page manifest for all later experiments.
2A. Sparse text indexing
Extract native PDF text per page with PyMuPDF.
Tokenize page text and build one BM25 entry per page.
Run BM25 retrieval directly on the shared page corpus.
2B. Multimodal image indexing
Render each page to an image cache.
Create full-page, fixed-crop, or layout-aware views.
Embed images with CLIP or SigLIP and add them to FAISS.
2C. Reusable experiment assets
manifest.jsonl, page_bm25.pkl
clip_faiss_full / fixed / layout
siglip_faiss_full / fixed / layout
3. Retrieval runs
For each question, retrieve the top 50 pages with a chosen method. BM25 uses lexical scoring; CLIP and SigLIP encode the query text and search the corresponding FAISS index.
For cropped multimodal indexes, individual crop or region hits are aggregated back to a page score by taking the highest matching crop for each page.
4A. Quantitative evaluation
Compute page Recall@1/5/10, page MRR, nDCG@10, document recall, and document MRR.
Persist metrics.json and ranked_pages.jsonl for each run.
4B. Analysis and report artifacts
Generate summary tables, recall curves, localization ablations, win/loss summaries, first-hit rank analyses, and qualitative retrieval comparisons.
These outputs drive the final report figures and conclusions.
Key design choice: expensive work happens during one-time indexing, while evaluation and analysis can be rerun repeatedly from saved indexes and ranked outputs.
